events.title: Khark 5 tanker fire/explosion and crude oil spill north of the Canary Islands
events.description: A source report describes an explosion/fire aboard the Iranian tanker Khark 5 on December 19, 1989, north of the Canary Islands, resulting in a major release of crude oil to the Atlantic Ocean and subsequent response activities.

location.name: Atlantic Ocean, north of the Canary Islands

events.title: Inuvik spill report: 400 liters of aviation gasoline from pipe/line leak
events.description: A Government of the Northwest Territories spill record reports a pipe/line leak in Inuvik, Northwest Territories, involving about 400 liters of aviation gasoline (Turbo B/Jet B) with an occurrence date listed in December 1989.

sources.source_name: Government of the Northwest Territories (ECC) Spill Database
sources.headline: Spill record spill-1989189

events.title: Isserk I-15 spill report: 400 liters of crude oil attributed to truck/unknown cause
events.description: A Government of the Northwest Territories spill record reports a crude oil release of about 400 liters at Isserk I-15 in the Beaufort Delta area in December 1989, with source noted as truck and cause unknown.

location.name: Isserk I-15, Beaufort Delta, Northwest Territories, Canada

sources.source_name: Government of the Northwest Territories (ECC) Spill Database
sources.headline: Spill record spill-1989197

events.title: Isserk I-15 spill report: 2,400 liters of crude oil from pipe/line leak
events.description: A Government of the Northwest Territories spill record reports a crude oil release of about 2,400 liters at Isserk I-15 in the Beaufort Delta area in December 1989, with the source and cause listed as a pipe/line leak.

location.name: Isserk I-15, Beaufort Delta, Northwest Territories, Canada

sources.source_name: Government of the Northwest Territories (ECC) Spill Database
sources.headline: Spill record spill-1989198

events.title: Christmas 1989 coastal snowstorm impacts along the U.S. Southeast Atlantic coast
events.description: A coastal winter storm produced significant snowfall along parts of the Southeast U.S. Atlantic coast shortly before Christmas 1989. Reports indicate measurable snow extended unusually far south, including into parts of Florida, alongside very cold conditions.

key_quotes.quote: record snowfall along the Southeast U.S. coast

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1989-12 US cold wave / Christmas 1989 storm cluster

events.title: Historic December 1989 Arctic outbreak across the central and eastern United States
events.description: A major Arctic outbreak in late December 1989 brought widespread record or near-record low temperatures across large parts of the central and eastern United States. Reports noted unusually deep freezes into the Southeast, including Florida, with impacts such as frozen pipes and agricultural damage.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1989-12 US cold wave / Christmas 1989 storm cluster

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Felicity affected northern Australia including Cape York Peninsula
events.description: A monsoon depression developed into Tropical Cyclone Felicity in December 1989, reaching peak intensity mid-month before crossing the Cape York Peninsula and later moving toward the Coral Sea. Available summaries describe impacts as limited, with mainly vegetation damage reported.

events.title: Intense lake-effect snow squalls affected snowbelts of western and north-central New York
events.description: An Arctic air mass crossing the Great Lakes produced intense lake-effect snow squalls in New York State, with heavy accumulations reported in areas influenced by Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. Reports cite notable totals in Oswego and Lewis counties and in snowbelt areas south of Buffalo.

events.title: Freeze-related crop damage reported across parts of the U.S. South after late-December cold
events.description: News reports following the late-December 1989 cold indicated severe damage to crops in parts of the U.S. South. Reported impacts included significant losses to Florida citrus and other winter vegetables after prolonged freezing temperatures.

sources.headline: Citrus, vegetable crops severely damaged in South

key_quotes.quote: Citrus, vegetable crops severely damaged in South

events.title: Ungava Peninsula, Québec earthquake (Ms 6.3) with documented surface faulting
events.description: An Ms 6.3 earthquake struck the Ungava Peninsula in northern Québec on 25 December 1989. Earthquakes Canada describes the event and notes documented surface faulting associated with the Lac Turquoise Fault Scarp.

events.title: Newcastle, New South Wales earthquake (1989)
events.description: An intraplate earthquake struck the Newcastle area of New South Wales in late December 1989, causing severe urban damage and fatalities. Geoscience Australia provides a feature summary of the event and its impacts.

sources.headline: 30 years on: commemorating the 1989 Newcastle earthquake

key_quotes.quote: 30 years on: commemorating the 1989 Newcastle earthquake

events.title: Strong earthquake reported near the north coast of Papua New Guinea (preliminary M 6.7)
events.description: A news report cited the U.S. Geological Survey describing a strong earthquake near Papua New Guinea’s north coast with a preliminary magnitude of 6.7. The report said there were no immediate reports of damage or casualties at the time.

sources.headline: Major earthquake strikes near Papua New Guinea

key_quotes.quote: ...citing the U.S. Geological Survey... preliminary magnitude of 6.7

events.title: Industrial fire at Acme Metal Finishing prompts evacuation in Glassell Park, Los Angeles
events.description: A fire at Acme Metal Finishing in the Glassell Park area of Los Angeles produced smoke described as toxic, leading authorities to evacuate nearby residents. The report cited concerns about chemical materials involved and noted weather conditions that kept smoke low to the ground.

location.name: Glassell Park, Los Angeles, California, USA

sources.headline: Toxic-smoke industrial fire at Acme Metal Finishing forces evacuation in Glassell Park (Los Angeles, California, USA)

dedupe.cluster_hint: Acme Metal Finishing Glassell Park fire 1989-12

events.title: Explosion and fires at Exxon Baton Rouge refinery ignite storage tanks
events.description: An explosion and subsequent fires at Exxon’s Baton Rouge refinery ignited storage tanks containing petroleum products, producing a large smoke plume and prompting emergency response. Reports described blast impacts in the surrounding area and continued firefighting activity.

sources.headline: Blast, Fires Wrack No. 2 U.S. Refinery
Exxon worker missing in refinery explosion fire
A look back at the years since the Exxon explosion in Baton Rouge

dedupe.cluster_hint: Exxon Baton Rouge refinery explosion and fires 1989-12-24

events.title: Report documents widespread coral bleaching in Jamaica beginning in late September 1989
events.description: A Nature correspondence (published Feb 1990) reported widespread coral bleaching affecting reef-building corals in Jamaica, beginning no earlier than late September 1989 and associated with elevated sea temperatures. The publication provides formal documentation of a biodiversity-impacting bleaching event continuing into late 1989.

events.title: Study reports morbillivirus linked to mass mortality in European harbour seals (>17,000 deaths)
events.description: A scientific article published in December 1989 reported that a morbillivirus was responsible for a major epizootic in harbour seals that began in April 1988 and spread across northern European waters. The report documented more than 17,000 seal deaths and contributed to ongoing scientific assessment of the outbreak's biodiversity impacts.

sources.headline: Morbillivirus and harbour seal mortality (article via DOI)
Repository record related to harbour seal epizootic / morbillivirus

key_quotes.quote: ...morbillivirus... responsible for a major epizootic in harbour seals...

events.title: North American Wetlands Conservation Act signed into law (U.S.)
events.description: The U.S. North American Wetlands Conservation Act was signed into law, creating a federal grants program administered by the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service to support wetlands conservation projects in North America.

sources.headline: Interior Secretary Lujan Praises Signing of North American Wetlands Conservation Act
Interior Secretary Lujan Praises Signing of North American Wetlands Conservation Act (December 13)

key_quotes.quote: praises signing of North American Wetlands Conservation Act

events.title: EPA issues Volatility Civil Penalty Policy for gasoline and alcohol blends (U.S.)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued a civil penalty policy describing how penalties would be assessed for violations of federal fuel volatility requirements for gasoline and alcohol blends, supporting enforcement of air pollution controls.

events.title: EPA Subpart W NESHAP final rule listed in rulemaking history (radon-222 emissions from uranium mill tailings)
events.description: EPA’s Subpart W rulemaking history references a final rule dated December 15, 1989 concerning National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants related to radon-222 emissions standards for operating uranium mill tailings.
